commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds read-only sandbox handling for workspace `skills` and `.agents/skills` roots, updates sandbox hash state, and adds focused mount/hash/bridge coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: PR head unconditionally adds remote protected mounts, while the Docker/generic resolver filters to existing directories. I did not run live Docker or remote sandbox commands because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: There is a narrow mechanical repair: make remote fs bridge protected skill-root resolution match Docker/generic and add focused regression coverage before maintainer compatibility review.

target.title: fix(sandbox): mount workspace skills read-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #17931.

This PR protects workspace skill paths exposed to sandboxes from in-sandbox writes.

In `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, the normal workspace remains writable, but these skill roots are protected:

- `<workspace>/skills` -> `/workspace/skills:ro,z`
- `<workspace>/.agents/skills` -> `/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z`

In `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `"none"`, the existing read-only `/workspace` mount provides the protection without adding a stale nested `/workspace/skills` bind.

## What changed

- Added read-only Docker overlays for workspace skill roots in `rw` mode.
- Kept `ro` and `none` on the existing read-only `/workspace` mount.
- Removed chmod-based copied-skill protection.
- Added stable skill-mount hash state so old containers are recreated when protected skill roots appear.
- Bumped `SANDBOX_MOUNT_FORMAT_VERSION` from `2` to `3`.
- Added bridge policy coverage so generic and remote fs bridge resolution also treats protected skill roots as read-only.
- Added tests for Docker mount behavior, hash behavior, and bridge read-only resolution.

## Security impact

This prevents sandboxed agents from rewriting the skill instructions they are executing under. Normal workspace writes in `rw` mode still work, but writes under protected skill paths now fail.

## Validation

Targeted command:

```text
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-skills-bridge-readonly.test.ts \
  src/agents/skills.build-workspace-skills-prompt.syncs-merged-skills-into-target-workspace.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Workspace skill instructions exposed to Docker sandboxes are protected from in-sandbox writes while the normal workspace remains writable. The generic and remote OpenClaw fs bridge write paths now also classify `/workspace/skills` and `/workspace/.agents/skills` as read-only, so OpenClaw file operations do not bypass the Docker mount policy.

**Real environment tested:** Repository openclaw/openclaw, PR #85591, branch jason-allen-oneal:fix/sandbox-readonly-skill-mounts, current PR head b5c4159c65f268087b2dc1bed94ff9d3872a63ad. Proof was run from the local repository using the current branch, Docker image openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim, a temporary workspace, the generic OpenClaw fs bridge, and the remote OpenClaw fs bridge runtime path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `.tmp-pr85591-bridge-proof.ts` from the PR branch. The script created a temporary workspace with `skills/demo/SKILL.md` and `.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md`, created a Docker sandbox with `/workspace` writable and both skill roots mounted read-only, wrote a normal workspace file through the generic OpenClaw fs bridge, attempted writes to both protected skill roots through the generic bridge, wrote a normal workspace file through the remote OpenClaw fs bridge, and attempted writes to both protected skill roots through the remote bridge.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the proof run:

```text
head=b5c4159c65f268087b2dc1bed94ff9d3872a63ad
workspace=/redacted/proof/workspace
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace/skills -> /workspace/skills RW=false
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace/.agents/skills -> /workspace/.agents/skills RW=false
docker mount: /redacted/proof/workspace -> /workspace RW=true
OK: generic OpenClaw fs bridge wrote normal workspace file
OK: generic bridge write to /workspace/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md
OK: generic bridge write to /workspace/.agents/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md
docker readback normal.txt=generic-normal-ok
OK: remote OpenClaw fs bridge wrote normal workspace file
OK: remote bridge write to /workspace/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-rtUyml/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md
OK: remote bridge write to /workspace/.agents/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-rtUyml/workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md
host readback remote-normal.txt=remote-normal-ok
RESULT: proof complete
```

**Observed result after fix:** Normal workspace writes succeeded through both the generic and remote OpenClaw fs bridges. Writes under `/workspace/skills` and `/workspace/.agents/skills` failed through both bridge paths with read-only sandbox path errors. The Docker mount table also showed `/workspace` writable while both skill roots were mounted read-only.

**What was not tested:** A full interactive UI session was not included. This proof covers the Docker mount behavior and both OpenClaw fs bridge write paths directly.
target.author: jason-allen-oneal

OK: remote bridge write to /workspace/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-rtUyml/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md
OK: remote bridge write to /workspace/.agents/skills blocked: Sandbox path is read-only; cannot write files: /tmp/openclaw-pr85591-proof-rtUyml/workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md
